businessSummary.oneLiner: Government and commercial AI data analytics platform leader with Gotham (defense), Foundry (enterprise), and AIP (AI platform)
businessSummary.description: Palantir builds AI data analytics platforms for government and enterprise. FY2025 revenue $4.48B (+60%). US commercial revenue exceeded $650M (Q4 +137% YoY). Pentagon adopted Maven AI as official program of record for military-wide deployment. Selected for Golden Dome $185B missile defense software. FY2026 guidance $7.19B-$7.41B (+60-65%).
howTheyMakeMoney: Enterprise AI platform (Foundry) - Large enterprise data integration
AIP (AI Platform) - Generative AI integration platform
AIP Bootcamps - Short-term adoption programs converting to customers

keyMetrics.name: Trailing P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: vs. S&P 500 IT sector avg 34x (6.7x premium). Extreme but gradually compressing with stock decline

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E (FY2026E)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on FY2026 EPS $1.32. Still elevated vs. growth stock average of 30-40x

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Growth (FY2026E)
keyMetrics.interpretation: From $4.48B to $7.40B projected. AI defense demand accelerating growth

keyMetrics.name: Insider Selling
keyMetrics.interpretation: Co-founder Thiel sold 2M shares in early March. Director sold 16K on 3/24. Significant insider selling pressure weighing on near-term sentiment

keyMetrics.name: Market Cap
keyMetrics.interpretation: Down 31% from 52-week high of $207. Golden Dome rally fully given back and then some

keyMetrics.name: RSI
keyMetrics.interpretation: Neutral zone. Room before oversold (30) but rebound momentum weak in current downtrend

growthDrivers.title: Trefis: '50%+ rally trigger factors' analysis (3/27)
growthDrivers.description: Trefis analyzed past PLTR 50%+ rallies. Current catalysts (Golden Dome, Maven) mirror patterns that preceded major moves

growthDrivers.title: Golden Dome $185B missile defense software selection (3/25)
growthDrivers.description: Palantir and Anduril selected to co-develop Trump administration's $185B Golden Dome missile defense system software. Long-term defense revenue pipeline secured

growthDrivers.title: Czech National Bank purchases PLTR (3/26)
growthDrivers.description: Czech central bank acquired 24,730 shares. Central bank buying an AI defense stock is a powerful institutional confidence signal

growthDrivers.title: Bain & Company global partnership expansion (3/26)
growthDrivers.description: Expanded partnership with Bain to accelerate global AIP/Foundry adoption. Consulting channel for enterprise market penetration

growthDrivers.title: Maven AI adopted as official military program of record (3/23)
growthDrivers.description: Pentagon designated Maven Smart System as official program of record. Long-term military-wide deployment confirmed

growthDrivers.title: FY2026 revenue guidance $7.19B-$7.41B (+60-65%)
growthDrivers.description: Management confident in growth acceleration. US commercial +137% YoY as core growth engine

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 20+ years of government defense AI track record, top-level security clearances, Maven military adoption, combat-proven in Iran conflict. Moats money cannot buy

moats.description: Ontology platform deeply integrated into government/military systems. Replacement costs prohibitively high
Top-level security clearances and 20+ year government trust. Virtually impossible for new entrants to replicate
AIP Bootcamps for rapid customer acquisition and Ontology ecosystem expansion
Used in thousands of real-time strikes during Iran conflict. Real combat data as competitive advantage

competitors.name: Anduril Industries
competitors.detail: Defense AI/drone startup. Collaborating with Palantir on Golden Dome. $60B valuation

competitors.name: Snowflake / Databricks
competitors.detail: Enterprise data platform competition. Significantly behind Palantir in government/defense access

competitors.name: Microsoft / AWS
competitors.detail: Cloud giants entering government AI. Scale advantage but lack Palantir's deep Ontology integration

competitors.name: C3.ai
competitors.detail: Enterprise AI platform. Far behind Palantir in both revenue and growth rate

recentNews.headline: Simply Wall St: 'Strong long-term returns amid short-term weakness, valuation reassessment'
recentNews.significance: Three consecutive declining sessions (-3.0% daily, -5.1% weekly) but long-term growth trajectory remains strong. P/E 227x as core debate

recentNews.headline: Peter Thiel sells 2M shares, heavy insider selling throughout March
recentNews.significance: Co-founder Thiel divested 2M shares in early March. Director sold 16K on 3/24. Insider selling creating near-term price pressure

recentNews.headline: PLTR -3% to $143.06, three straight declining sessions
recentNews.significance: Below both 50-day ($148.62) and 200-day ($168.58). Golden Dome rally fully given back

recentNews.headline: Trefis: 'What could send Palantir stock soaring 50%+'
recentNews.significance: Analysis of past major rally patterns finds current catalysts (Golden Dome, Maven) similar to historical triggers

recentNews.headline: Czech National Bank buys 24,730 shares + Bain partnership expansion
recentNews.significance: Central bank buying AI defense stock signals institutional confidence. Bain partnership accelerates global AIP adoption

analystOpinions.notableComment: 17 Buy, 14 Hold, 5 Sell. Consensus $199 (+39%). Rosenblatt maintains $200 Buy despite Thiel selling. Insider selling vs analyst optimism creating tension

risks.title: Extreme valuation premium
risks.description: P/E 227x (6.7x S&P IT avg of 34x), P/S 76x. Forward PE 108x still 3x growth stock average. Expectations excessively priced in

risks.title: Peter Thiel 2M share sale, accelerating insider selling
risks.description: Co-founder divested 2M shares in early March. Director sold 16K. Some institutions trimming positions. Insider selling weighs on near-term confidence

risks.title: Below both 50-day and 200-day SMAs
risks.description: 50-day ($148.62) and 200-day ($168.58) both above. Three consecutive down days. $126 support open below

risks.title: Next week macro headwinds
risks.description: Citigroup cutting US equity exposure. Nonfarm payrolls (4/3). Iran conflict. Particularly hostile environment for high-valuation tech

risks.title: Analyst opinion split
risks.description: 17 Buy, 14 Hold, 5 Sell. Market skepticism on valuation persists

buyReasons.title: Irreplaceable AI defense position + long-term pipeline
buyReasons.rationale: Golden Dome $185B + Maven program of record + combat-proven. Czech central bank buying. Irreplaceable in defense AI

buyReasons.title: Explosive commercial growth + global expansion
buyReasons.rationale: US commercial Q4 +137%. Bain partnership accelerating global AIP adoption. FY2026 +65% guidance

buyReasons.title: 31% discount from highs
buyReasons.rationale: Down 31% from $207 52-week high. Fundamentals strengthened by Golden Dome and Maven while price declined

buyReasons.title: Trefis 50%+ rally catalyst analysis
buyReasons.rationale: Current catalysts mirror past patterns that preceded major rallies. Rebound amplitude could be significant

overallOpinion: Peter Thiel's sale of 2 million shares in early March has been newly confirmed. A director also sold 16K shares. The co-founder's large divestiture raises 'what do insiders know?' questions and is pressuring near-term sentiment
But fundamentals are at all-time strength. Golden Dome $185B, Maven program of record, Czech central bank buying, Bain partnership. Trefis sees 50%+ rally catalyst patterns building
Next week's nonfarm payrolls and macro environment could further pressure high-valuation tech. P/E 227x with 17 Buy, 14 Hold, 5 Sell shows the market remains divided
$143.06, RSI 45. Below 50-day ($149) and 200-day ($169). Down 31% from highs. Hold, small accumulation valid but monitor insider selling trends

investmentVerdict.summary: Thiel's 2M share sale and accelerating insider selling create near-term headwinds. But Golden Dome, Maven, and commercial +137% make fundamentals strongest ever. P/E 227x with macro headwinds means heavy short-term pressure. Monitor insider selling trends while small position accumulation remains valid

x.hook: PLTR $143 | Peter Thiel sold 2M shares. What does the insider know?
x.text:
$PLTR $143.06 (weekend, flat)

Peter Thiel sold 2M shares in early March. A director sold 16K more. Co-founder's mass divestiture.

Consensus $199 (+39%)

1/4
thread:
But fundamentals are strongest ever:
Golden Dome $185B, Maven adopted.
Czech central bank buying. Bain partnership.

Trefis: 50%+ rally catalysts building

2/4

Two readings of insider selling:
1. 'Insiders know something' (negative)
2. 'Portfolio diversification' (neutral)

Thiel also closing Founders Fund $6B.
Reduced NVDA, TSLA too

3/4

P/E 227x. Below 50-day ($149) and 200-day ($169).
Down 31% from highs.

Hold. Monitor insider activity. Small positions only.

4/4

threads.hook: PLTR: Peter Thiel sold 2M shares, yet fundamentals are at all-time strength
threads.text:
$PLTR $143.06 (weekend)

Peter Thiel sold 2M shares in early March. Director sold 16K on 3/24. Co-founder's large divestiture weighs on sentiment.

But Golden Dome $185B, Maven program of record, Czech central bank buying, Bain partnership. Trefis sees 50%+ rally patterns building. Consensus $199 (+39%).

P/E 227x, below both moving averages. Thiel may be diversifying for Founders Fund. Hold, small positions only.

witty.hook: Peter Thiel built the world's best defense AI, then sold 2M shares. Hmm.
witty.text:
$PLTR $143.06 (weekend)

Peter Thiel, co-founder, sold 2M shares.
The man who built the world's top defense AI is selling his own stock.

But this company just won Golden Dome, right?

1/4
thread:
Thiel is also reducing NVDA and TSLA.
Closing Founders Fund $6B. Needs cash.

Insider selling doesn't always mean bad news.
But it never feels good either.

2/4

The bull case is absurdly strong:
Golden Dome, Maven, central bank buying, Bain.

The problem? P/E 227x.
50 catalysts can't move a stock this expensive.

3/4

Down 31% from highs. Consensus $199.
Trefis: 50%+ rally possible.

Hold. Can't outsmart Peter Thiel.

4/4

empathy.hook: PLTR holders, the Thiel selling news is unsettling. Let's look at the full picture.
empathy.text:
$PLTR $143.06 (weekend)

Peter Thiel selling 2M shares. He's the co-founder. Naturally that's alarming.

But the full picture may not be as bad as it seems

1/4
thread:
Thiel is raising Founders Fund $6B.
Also reducing NVDA, TSLA.
Likely portfolio diversification, not abandonment.

Golden Dome and Maven are still here.

2/4

Czech central bank is buying. Trefis sees 50%+ rally.
Fundamentals haven't cracked.

Insider selling is concerning but the company's direction is clear.

3/4

Consensus $199 (+39%). Down 31% from highs.
Don't act impulsively.
Small positions, manage risk.

Hold. Time will clarify.

4/4

Peter Thiel, co-founder, 2M shares sold. Also cutting NVDA, TSLA. Raising Founders Fund $6B.

Golden Dome $185B won but the founder is selling. Bull case has never been stronger yet the co-founder is cashing out. P/E 227x makes everyone nervous.

empathy.hook: PLTR shareholders, the Thiel selling is worrying. But look at the full context.
empathy.text:
$PLTR $143.06, down 31% from highs.

Peter Thiel selling 2M shares is scary when he's the co-founder. It's natural to worry.

But he's also raising Founders Fund $6B and reducing positions across NVDA and TSLA too. This looks like diversification, not abandonment.

Golden Dome $185B, Maven adopted, commercial +137%. Trefis sees 50%+ rally patterns. Consensus $199 (+39%).

Small positions, stay patient. The fundamentals haven't changed.
